title: Grease on Cotton
stainChemistry: 'Grease' is a catch-all for thick animal or vegetable fats that coat cotton — bacon grease, butter, lard, chicken fat, cooking-pan splatter. These are triglycerides similar to cooking oil but thicker, more viscous, and more adherent at room temperature. Fresh grease sits on fiber surfaces; aged grease oxidizes and polymerizes (same linseed-oil-varnish chemistry as aged cooking oil). Cotton tolerates POG, mineral spirits, Dawn, and hot wash. For motor oil specifically see motor-oil-cotton; for light cooking oil see cooking-oil-cotton.
whyThisWorks: Powder preload pulls bulk grease out of the fiber via capillary action — no chemistry, no heat, effective. Dawn is the wildlife-rehab standard for triglyceride emulsion and works on most kitchen grease. For aged / polymerized grease, mineral spirits or POG tamp re-softens the hardened film before Dawn emulsifies it.
defaultAssumption: Kitchen / restaurant context. Fresh bacon / butter / pan-splatter = high-confidence DIY with powder + Dawn. Aged grease trends harder. Hot-dryer grease polymerizes into a shiny film that resists further Dawn treatment.

spottingProtocol.technique: Wick grease out via capillary action
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cover the grease stain with a thick layer of cornstarch, baking soda, or fuller's earth. Let sit 20–30 min. The powder wicks thick grease UP out of the fiber with no water needed. Brush off.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn dish soap or NSD + warm water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply a generous amount of Dawn or NSD directly to the stain. Dawn is formulated specifically for triglyceride emulsification. Work with a soft toothbrush for 1–2 minutes. Dwell 5 min. Rinse warm.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Mineral spirits or POG on clean white cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: For aged grease with a shiny, stiff film (usually hot-dried): tamp mineral spirits or POG on a white cloth from the back. Ventilate. Flip cloth frequently. Follow with another Dawn pass to clear the solvent.
Launder hot per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer — any residual grease polymerizes under dryer heat into a semi-permanent film.

steps: 1. Cornstarch / fuller's earth preload 20–30 min. Brush off.
2. Generous NSD / Dawn + soft brush, 5 min.
3. Aged / polymerized: mineral spirits tamp from back.
professionalProtocol.temperature: room temp through spotting; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Powder preload cuts work in half

homeSolutions.instruction: Cover the grease with cornstarch, baking soda, or plain baby powder. Let it sit 20–30 minutes. The powder wicks the grease UP out of the fabric — no scrubbing, no chemistry. Brush off with a soft brush. Much of the stain usually comes with it.

Apply a generous amount of Dawn dish soap directly to the stain. Work it in with a fingertip or soft toothbrush for 1–2 minutes. Dawn is what wildlife rehabbers use for oil-spill cleanup — it's specifically designed for grease. Let it sit 5 minutes, rinse warm. Repeat if you can feel any oily residue.

If the grease has been through a hot dryer and has a stiff / shiny film, soap alone won't touch it. Tamp mineral spirits (paint thinner, any hardware store) on a clean white rag onto the spot from the BACK. Ventilate. No flames. Then repeat the Dawn step.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder on the hottest setting the care label allows. Inspect BEFORE the dryer — any residual grease polymerizes under heat into a near-permanent film.

steps: 1. Cornstarch / baby powder preload 20–30 min. Brush off.
2. Generous Dawn + soft toothbrush, 5 min, rinse warm. Repeat.
3. Aged grease: mineral spirits on a rag from the back. Ventilate.
4. Hot wash per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if grease has been hot-dried and two home rounds don't clear the polymerized film.
warnings: Inspect BEFORE the dryer — heat sets residual grease

neverDo: Never use solvent near open flame
Never pour solvent directly — tamp with cloth
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates POG, mineral spirits, Dawn, NSD, hot wash
Dyed cotton: mineral spirits usually safe; inside-seam-test if concerned

materialWarnings: Powder first cuts the work in half

escalation.when: Grease hot-dried into a polymerized film and two home rounds don't clear it.
escalation.whatToTell: "Grease on cotton, age [fresh / aged / hot-dried polymerized], home-treated with cornstarch + Dawn + [mineral spirits]."

contextQuestions: Has it been through a hot dryer?
Kitchen / cooking grease, or motor / mechanical grease? (motor-oil-cotton for the latter)
Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Fresh kitchen grease is a powder + Dawn win. Dryer-polymerized grease is the multi-step variant. Motor oil has its own card; see motor-oil-cotton.
